top_concerns.concern: official T-75 lineups missing and player-state still partial
top_concerns.evidence: tactics-coach.json says official_t_minus_75_lineups unavailable; player-state files still require final recheck
top_concerns.why_it_matters: third-round rotation, minutes limits, and last-minute absences can materially move xG and score tails

top_concerns.concern: official group table and tiebreaker snapshot missing in current thread
top_concerns.evidence: data-collector.json still shows standings_pending; current table is inferred from prior match results, not sourced here
top_concerns.why_it_matters: third-round motivation can be misread if top spot, draw acceptance, or third-place value is not explicitly sourced
top_concerns.follow_up_check: add official table, tiebreaker, and qualification-state labels for all four teams

top_concerns.concern: France brand premium may inflate baseline more than evidence supports
top_concerns.evidence: team-level red-team review already flagged France prestige premium; live market snapshot is still missing
top_concerns.why_it_matters: France win rate and 2+ goal margin can be overstated if France are more willing to manage a draw or rotate
top_concerns.follow_up_check: check rotation signs, pace-management signals, and whether live odds still overprice France name value

top_concerns.concern: Norway high-score tail is real but easy to overfit
top_concerns.evidence: round1 and round2 postmortems both said Norway scoring ceiling was previously underestimated after games open up
top_concerns.why_it_matters: model can fail in two directions: too narrow if tail not widened, too aggressive if 4-1 and 3-2 are treated as stable baseline
top_concerns.follow_up_check: recheck Norway open-game scoring ceiling and defensive exposure in a first-place chase script

top_concerns.concern: Senegal and Iraq samples remain shallow and context-sensitive
top_concerns.evidence: team red-team reviews flagged sample bias, opponent-quality issues, and instability in attack and defense assumptions
top_concerns.why_it_matters: single-scoreline and prestige-based gaps can overstate Senegal and understate Iraq draw or upset paths
top_concerns.follow_up_check: add opponent-quality adjustment, split recent samples, and clarify round3 incentive context

top_concerns.concern: same-book same-time executable market snapshot missing
top_concerns.why_it_matters: stale odds can mislead calibration, especially in round3 when lineup and motivation news move fast
top_concerns.follow_up_check: capture 1X2, handicap, and totals from the same source with timestamp

top_concerns.concern: independent Poisson is likely too narrow for red cards, early goals, chase-state, and simultaneous-match strategy shifts
top_concerns.evidence: round2 postmortem already flagged static Poisson as too narrow for Norway vs Senegal; round3 adds simultaneous-kickoff dependency
top_concerns.why_it_matters: BTTS, late flips, and sudden tempo changes can be underweighted
top_concerns.follow_up_check: expand score-tail treatment or add explicit manual uncertainty adjustment

upset_or_failure_scenarios.scenario: France accept draw management or partial rotation while Norway push harder for first place
an early Norway goal or weaker France protection opens the match and widens BTTS and 2-2 or 1-2 paths
Senegal struggle to break a low block and Iraq steal one from a set piece or counter
simultaneous-match information changes late-game urgency and breaks a low-total baseline

overconfidence_flags: do not project France two-win momentum directly into full-intensity round3 motivation
do not extrapolate Norway high scores linearly into a permanent shootout baseline
do not treat Senegal prestige as enough evidence to suppress draw and Iraq scoring share
without T-75 lineups and final injury news, any exact score framing is overconfident
market_flags: France public-team premium remains a live risk
round3 odds may be stale if they are not same-book same-time captured
if market chases Norway recent goals or shortens France only on name value, calibration should be conservative
quant_chain_flags: no sourced third_round_context or qualification_scenarios in current thread
no poisson_score_matrix to verify whether heavy-tail widening was applied

direction: slightly up Norway scoring and BTTS paths
down confidence in France by 2 or more goals
recommended_probability_adjustment.reason: France brand premium and two-win narrative can overstate motivation; Norway first-place incentive and scoring tail remain live

direction: down Senegal win probability and margin confidence
slightly up Iraq scoring and upset paths
widen score range instead of defaulting to a low-total script
recommended_probability_adjustment.reason: sample quality is weak, game-state volatility is meaningful, and Senegal reputation can be overpriced

handoff_notes: use this as a red-team gate only. data must add official table, tiebreaker, odds, injuries, and card status. tactics must clarify rotation and tempo assumptions. model must add the full round3 quant file and avoid double-counting brand, motivation, and market signals.
